commit.message:
Implement theme system (Light / Dark / System default)

Wires the existing Settings > Theme dropdown to actual visual changes:
- Four CoffeeSettings colour accessors (ThemePanelBg, ThemeTextColor,
  ThemeDimTextColor, ThemeOutlineColor) return palette values per theme
- SyncAllWindows() broadcasts MSG_THEME_CHANGED to all open windows
- Custom Draw() methods (ParticleGaugeView, SieveDistView, CalHistView,
  ExtractionBarView, RoastGaugeView, ThumbView) use theme colours for
  backgrounds, outlines, tick marks, and labels
- BTextView tips areas update view/text colour on MSG_THEME_CHANGED and
  in constructors so newly opened windows inherit the active theme
- Coffee-colour gradients and extraction zone colours are unchanged

Add README

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix duplicate institutes, magistratura normalization, and add full reset

- Fix INSTITUTE_MAP key mismatch: comma-space in key caused 7th phantom
  institute. Now strips commas, dots, spaces for consistent matching
- Add fuzzy keyword fallback for institute name detection
- Add Full Reset button in admin import page (deletes all structure + lessons)
- Replace heavy blur page transitions with lightweight opacity+y animations
- Add global ErrorBoundary to catch runtime crashes gracefully
- Remove debug console.log spam from schedule, structure, and client

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Proxy API through CloudFront to fix India connectivity

Indian users could load the frontend but API calls failed because
direct requests to lambda-url.us-east-1.on.aws were blocked by some
ISPs. Route /api/* through the same CloudFront distribution so all
requests are same-origin.

- Add Lambda as second CloudFront origin with /api/* path behavior
- Upgrade price class to PriceClass_200 (India/Asia/ME/Africa edges)
- Set origin_read_timeout to 60s for long-running predictions
- Use relative API paths in frontend (empty NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL)
- Break circular Terraform module dependency (frontend ↔ lambda)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: UI reactiveness overhaul - faster animation tokens and snappier interactions

Implement all 12 user stories (US-001 through US-012) for improved perceived
responsiveness. Add instant/quick duration tokens and snappy easing curve.
Reduce transition durations across all UI components (buttons, inputs, cards,
toggles, selects, modals, toasts, tooltips, tabs, sidebar) from 200-300ms
to 50-150ms. Add spring physics to toggle knob and tab indicators. Fix
pre-existing lint issues in Toast component.

commit.message:
refactor: 機率模型評價區塊大幅細化

- 綜合評分（100 分制，多維度加權）取代單一評級
- 5 欄 KPI：綜合評分 / 評級 / 得標命中比 / 收入誤差 / 月度校準率
- 新增分析維度：
  1. 得標節奏（前後半段分布）
  2. 單次平均收入比較 + 得標時機解讀
  3. 月度 MAE（收入穩定性）
  4. 各組表現摘要（符合/超出/落後 + top 3 明細）
  5. 模型穩定性漂移檢測（前後半誤差趨勢）
- 建議依情境觸發，涵蓋期間長度/得標速度/收入偏差/校準率/漂移

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use standard setuptools build backend for compatibility

The previous `setuptools.backends._legacy:_Backend` is only available
in very recent setuptools versions. Switch to the standard
`setuptools.build_meta` which works everywhere.

어드민 데모 (Supabase 미연결 시):
- 12개 예시 케이스 추가 (접수됨/수거예정/판매중/정산완료/취소됨 전 상태)

이용후기 캐러셀:
- 끝에서 다음 클릭 시 처음으로 루프
- 처음에서 이전 클릭 시 끝으로 루프 (양방향 무한순환)
- 화살표 버튼 항상 활성화

트럭 드래그 로켓 부스터:
- 릴리즈 시 단일 🔥 버스트 애니메이션 (확대→페이드)
- 트럭 후방 단일 불꽃 엘리먼트 (CSS gradient, flicker 애니메이션)
- 귀환 속도 0.14 → 0.04 (천천히 부드럽게 복귀)
- 간헐적 파티클 제거 → 단일 지속 불꽃으로 교체

CTA 섹션:

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: route admin settings API through proxy instead of page URL

getAdminSettings() was fetching `/admin/settings` which resolved to the
Next.js settings PAGE (returns HTML), not the Workers API. This caused
JSON parse failure and the "Failed to fetch settings from API, trying
localStorage" console warning on every admin page load.

Fix: change endpoint to `/api/proxy/admin/settings` which routes through
the catch-all proxy with ADMIN_TOKEN injection and tenantId forwarding.
Also added optional tenantId parameter to both get/update functions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(pubsub): implement Iteration 69 — Sharded Pub/Sub (Redis 7.0+)

Implemented sharded pub/sub commands for cluster-mode support:
- SSUBSCRIBE: Subscribe to sharded channels (hash-slot routed)
- SUNSUBSCRIBE: Unsubscribe from sharded channels (all or specific)
- SPUBLISH: Publish to sharded channel
- PUBSUB SHARDCHANNELS: List active sharded channels with pattern filter
- PUBSUB SHARDNUMSUB: Get subscriber counts for sharded channels

Storage layer:
- Extended PubSub struct with sharded_channels HashMap
- Extended SubscriberState with sharded_channels set
- Added ssubscribe/sunsubscribe/sunsubscribeAll/spublish methods
- Added activeShardedChannels, shardedChannelSubscriberCount helpers
- Sharded channels are independent from regular channels and patterns

Frame builders:
- buildSmessageFrame: smessage format for sharded pub/sub
- buildSsubscribeFrame: ssubscribe confirmation
- buildSunsubscribeFrame: sunsubscribe confirmation

Command handlers:
- cmdSsubscribe: Handles SSUBSCRIBE with multi-channel support
- cmdSunsubscribe: Handles SUNSUBSCRIBE (all or specific channels)
- cmdSpublish: Publishes to sharded channel, returns subscriber count
- cmdPubsubShardchannels: Lists active sharded channels with glob pattern filter
- cmdPubsubShardnumsub: Returns alternating channel names and subscriber counts

Tests:
- 16 unit tests for sharded pub/sub storage layer
- 10 unit tests for command handlers
- All tests pass with zero memory leaks

Documentation:
- Updated CLAUDE.md with Iteration 69
- Updated project memory with completion status

Completes Phase 4 pub/sub feature set (9/9 commands, 100%)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: prefix public asset paths with BASE_URL for GitHub Pages

Hardcoded /images/... and /omer-profile.png paths were missing the
/omeretrog-website/ subpath on GitHub Pages. Use import.meta.env.BASE_URL
so paths work on both local dev (/) and GitHub Pages (/omeretrog-website/).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ego-field-test): indent multi-line expressions in json_extract wrapper

The json_extract helper (introduced in 685d3dc) wraps Python expressions
in try/except but relied on template whitespace to indent the first line.
Multi-line expressions had subsequent lines at column 0, outside the try
block, causing IndentationError for all but the first few extensions.

Pipe expressions through sed to indent every line by 4 spaces.

commit.message:
Fix _stat_ts crash: handle float Unix timestamps from recorder

HA recorder can return stat["start"] as a float (Unix timestamp) on some
versions. The previous code fell through to dt = ts (a float) and then
crashed on dt.replace(tzinfo=...) with 'float has no attribute replace'.

Now handles int/float, str (ISO), datetime, and unknown types safely.

commit.message:
fix: ensure nix run uses authenticated GitHub API requests (#341)

* fix: ensure nix run uses authenticated GitHub API requests

The Nix installer's `access-tokens` config may not persist on
self-hosted runners with pre-installed Nix. Explicitly set
`access-tokens` via NIX_CONFIG before `nix run nixpkgs#gh` to
avoid unauthenticated rate limits (60/hr → 5000/hr).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use pnpm dlx for netlify-cli to fix chalk v5 ESM resolution

bunx flat-installs deps, hoisting chalk@5 (ESM-only) over chalk@^4
needed by the CJS chain inquirer → ora → log-symbols. Under Node.js
v24, this causes `TypeError: chalk.blue is not a function`. pnpm dlx
properly isolates conflicting versions in nested node_modules.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use explicit pnpm dlx syntax for netlify-cli multi-binary package

pnpm dlx errors when a package has multiple binaries (ntl, netlify).
Use `pnpm --package=netlify-cli dlx netlify` to disambiguate.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: add TODO with upstream issue link for netlify-cli bunx workaround

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
